name: Hippopotamus
summary: Hippos are huge river mammals with barrel-shaped bodies and enormous mouths. They spend the day in water to stay cool, graze on grass at night, and live in groups called pods across African rivers and lakes.
metaTitle: Hippopotamus Facts | Habitat, Diet, Pods & Photos
metaDescription: Discover hippopotamus facts with photos, river habitat, grazing diet, calf life, and conservation. Learn where hippos live, what they eat, and why they stay in water.
speed: Up to 19 mph on land in short bursts
adaptations: eyes and nostrils on top of head
funFacts: Hippos can hold their breath for several minutes underwater.
Despite living in water, hippos cannot swim—they push off the bottom or walk.
Hippo sweat looks reddish and may help protect their skin from sun and germs.
A hippo's mouth can open nearly 180 degrees during a threat display.
Hippos graze for hours at night and may eat more than 80 pounds of grass.
Baby hippos are born underwater and must swim to the surface to breathe.

faq.question: What is a hippopotamus?
faq.answer: A hippopotamus, often called a hippo, is a huge plant-eating mammal that lives in African rivers and lakes. Hippos spend much of the day in water.

faq.question: Where do hippos live?
faq.answer: Wild hippos live in rivers, lakes, and wetlands across sub-Saharan Africa. They need deep water for daytime resting and nearby grassland for feeding.

faq.question: What do hippos eat?
faq.answer: Hippos are herbivores that mainly eat grass. They leave the water at night to graze on land.

faq.question: Are hippos dangerous?
faq.answer: Yes. Hippos are unpredictable and very strong. They can charge boats or people who get too close, especially on land or near calves.

faq.question: Why do hippos stay in water?
faq.answer: Water keeps their huge bodies cool under the hot African sun and helps protect them from biting insects and some predators.

faq.question: Are hippos endangered?
faq.answer: Hippos are listed as Vulnerable. Hunting and habitat loss have reduced their numbers in many countries.

faq.question: What is a baby hippo called?
faq.answer: A baby hippo is called a calf. Calves stay close to their mothers for protection and nursing.
